LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JASON MICHAEL PAUL PRODUCTIONS INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FIRST, THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP SEC. 473.5(B), WHICH PROVIDES THAT THE MOTION "SHALL BE ACCOMPANIED BY AN AFFIDAVIT SHOWING UNDER OATH THAT THE PARTY'S LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE IN TIME TO DEFEND THE ACTION WAS NOT CAUSED BY HIS OR HER AVOIDANCE OF SERVICE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT" AND A PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING. DEFENDANT FILED ITS MOTION ON AUGUST 19, 2014, BUT DID NOT FILE ITS AFFIDAVIT AND PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING UNTIL SEPTEMBER 9, 2014, LESS THAN THE 16 COURT DAYS REQUIRED BY CCP SEC. 1005. SECOND, THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT DEFENDANT WAS PERSONALLY SERVED WITH THE ORDER FOR DEBTOR'S EXAMINATION ON FEBRUARY 8, 2014 AND THEREFORE KNEW ABOUT THIS CASE ON THAT DATE. (DECLARATION OF LORNA WALKER, PAR. 12, EX. C; DECLARATION OF KRIS VORSATZ, PAR. 3.) DEFENDANT DID NOT MOVE TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT UNTIL AUGUST 19, 2014, OVER SIX MONTHS LATER. THE MOTION WAS NOT BROUGHT WITHIN A "REASONABLE TIME" AS REQUIRED BY CCP SEC. 473.5(A). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185 (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-29-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICHOLAS K. JAY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF GILBERT JAY, TEL. NO. 788-7300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE COURT AND THE OPPOSING PARTY TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JASON MICHAEL PAUL PRODUCTIONS INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is Denied. First, the motion does not comply with CCP sec. 473.5(b), which provides that the motion "shall be accompanied by an affidavit showing under oath that the party's lack of actual notice in time to defend the action was not caused by his or her avoidance of service or excusable neglect" and a proposed responsive pleading. Defendant filed its motion on August 19, 2014, but did not file its affidavit and proposed responsive pleading until September 9, 2014, less than the 16 court days required by CCP sec. 1005. Second, the evidence establishes that Defendant was personally served with the Order for Debtor's Examination on February 8, 2014 and therefore knew about this case on that date. (Declaration of Lorna Walker, par. 12, Ex. C; Declaration of Kris Vorsatz, par. 3.) Defendant did not move to set aside the default and default judgment until August 19, 2014, over six months later. The motion was not brought within a "reasonable time" as required by CCP sec. 473.5(a). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/eg
